summary: Rake angle describes the inclination formed by the rake face against a radial reference; it pertains specifically to metal-cutting operations where this geometric relationship influences material removal efficiency and tool wear characteristics. To directly answer your query about terminology used for such angles within machining contexts, "rake angle" fits perfectly as per standard definitions provided across various engineering texts and standards documents related to machine tools design and operation procedures.
